observances.name: Ekadashi
observances.description: Fast (avoid grains). Recite Vishnu Sahasranama or Om Namo Narayanaya 108×. Read a chapter of Gita. Avoid late-night activities.

observances.name: Pradosham
observances.description: Twilight puja around local sunset. Recite Om Namah Shivaya 108×. Offer milk or bilva leaves if possible. Light a lamp facing west.

verse.devanagari: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
verse.transliteration: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana | mā karmaphalaheturbhūr mā te saṅgo'stvakarmaṇi ||
verse.meaning: You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action. Never consider yourself the cause of the results, and never be attached to inaction.

verse.devanagari: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत। अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
verse.transliteration: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata | abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṃ sṛjāmy aham ||
verse.meaning: Whenever there is a decline in righteousness and an increase in unrighteousness, O Arjuna, at that time I manifest Myself.

verse.devanagari: उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्। आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥
verse.transliteration: uddhared ātmanātmānaṃ nātmānam avasādayet | ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ ||
verse.meaning: Elevate yourself through the power of your own mind, and do not degrade yourself. The mind is the friend of the self, and the mind is the enemy of the self.

verse.devanagari: विश्वं विष्णुर्वषट्कारो भूतभव्यभवत्प्रभुः। भूतकृद्भूतभृद्भावो भूतात्मा भूतभावनः॥
verse.transliteration: viśvaṃ viṣṇur vaṣaṭkāro bhūtabhavyabhavatprabhuḥ | bhūtakṛd bhūtabhṛd bhāvo bhūtātmā bhūtabhāvanaḥ ||
verse.meaning: He who is the universe, who is all-pervading, the Lord of the past, present and future, the creator and sustainer of all beings.

verse.devanagari: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज। अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
verse.transliteration: sarvadharmān parityajya mām ekaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja | ahaṃ tvā sarvapāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ ||
verse.meaning: Abandon all varieties of dharma and simply surrender unto Me alone. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions — do not fear.

verse.devanagari: ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्। उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय माऽमृतात्॥
verse.transliteration: oṃ tryambakaṃ yajāmahe sugandhiṃ puṣṭivardhanam | urvārukam iva bandhanān mṛtyor mukṣīya mā'mṛtāt ||
verse.meaning: We worship the three-eyed Lord (Shiva) who is fragrant and who nourishes all beings. May He release us from death as a ripe cucumber falls from its vine, and may we never be separated from immortality.

verse.devanagari: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते। तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्॥
verse.transliteration: ananyāś cintayanto māṃ ye janāḥ paryupāsate | teṣāṃ nityābhiyuktānāṃ yogakṣemaṃ vahāmy aham ||
verse.meaning: To those who worship Me alone, thinking of no other, to those ever-united in thought, I secure what they lack and preserve what they have.

verse_of_week.devanagari: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
verse_of_week.transliteration: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana | mā karmaphalaheturbhūr mā te saṅgo'stvakarmaṇi ||
verse_of_week.meaning: You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action. Never consider yourself the cause of the results, and never be attached to inaction.

lifestyle_recommendations: Recite Lakshmi Ashtakam or Devi mantra. Keep the home clean and fragrant.
Recite Hanuman Chalisa or a Shiva mantra. Donate sesame / mustard oil. Serve someone in need.
Recite Gayatri Mantra 108× at sunrise. Offer water to the Sun (Surya Arghya). Eat lightly.
Fast (avoid grains). Recite Vishnu Sahasranama or Om Namo Narayanaya 108×. Read a chapter of Gita. Avoid late-night activities.
Recite Ganesha shloka before any important work. Avoid starting new ventures. Be patient.
